content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client clearly shifts across the 15 turns. She begins with an intellectualized worry ('How do I stop questioning if I'm showing up just to have my voice be heard, or if I truly have the thing to say?') and a self-judging frame ('Maybe I feel like I don't have valuable input'). By turn 9 she smiles and names the actual fear underneath ('that felt like that's the thing I'm afraid of'), recognizing her lack of control. By turn 13, when asked to look Joe in the eye and say 'I want to control what you think of me,' she does it and reports 'it feels ridiculous' — a somatic/affective shift where the control-seeking is felt, not just understood. Her closing 'totally absurd. I totally get it' (turn 15) shows the scorecard frame has dissolved into laughter. This meets the breakthrough condition: the coach did not reassure, the client herself touched the control-seeking and felt its absurdity.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 9, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client opens with intellectualized self-monitoring question — surface presentation."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client states the scorecard belief: 'I don't have valuable input.'"},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client begins to see the question isn't answerable — first crack in the frame."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'I don't know why I think it's a problem' — the premise loosens."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Smile + naming the actual fear ('that's the thing I'm afraid of') — affective shift, deeper layer surfaces."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Embodied experiment lands: 'it feels ridiculous' — control-seeking is felt, not analyzed."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Totally absurd. I totally get it' — frame has dissolved; closes with genuine thanks."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["invert-the-question", "dissolve-the-frame-with-mortality", "embodied-experiment", "self-as-fellow-traveler-not-authority", "whats-the-case-against-it", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "self-importance-as-defense"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Breakthrough condition met cleanly — coach refused to reassure, client found the absurdity herself."
}
